zh.lifeSummary: 當Ann Parham Smith於1635年8月出生在英國英格蘭時，她的父親William Isaac Smith20歲，母親Margrett Ann Parkham15歲。她於1660年與William Pettipoole Sr.在美國維吉尼亞州約克結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1727年4月20日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利享年91歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ann Parham Smith于1635年8月出生在英国英格兰时，她的父亲William Isaac Smith20岁，母亲Margrett Ann Parkham15岁。她于1660年与William Pettipoole Sr.在美国弗吉尼亚州約克结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1727年4月20日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smithe
meanings: English: variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smythe
meanings: English (London): variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: English:
occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

nameMeanings.name: Parham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from any of the places in Suffolk and Sussex, named in Old English with pere ‘pear’ + hām ‘homestead’ or hamm ‘water meadow’. Compare Parm .

variants.name: Parm
meanings: vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Perrien
meanings: English (Devon): variant of Perriam, a habitational name from a lost or unidentified place called from Middle English piri(e) ‘pear tree’ + ham(me) ‘enclosed plot, (water) meadow’. This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland. Compare Parham .
Breton: status name from a regional variant of perc'henn ‘owner (of a house)’.

variants.name: Perham
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from a place in Hertfordshire, so called from the Old English personal name Pēo(t)la + hām ‘homestead’. From 1715 a family bearing this surname held the dukedom of Newcastle.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

en.lifeSummary: When Ann Parham Smith was born in August 1635, in England, United Kingdom, her father, William Isaac Smith, was 20 and her mother, Margrett Ann Parkham, was 15. She married William Pettipoole Sr. in 1660, in York, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 20 April 1727, in Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 91.

Elle a épousé William Pettipoole Sr. en 1660, à York, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 avril 1727, à Surry, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 91 ans.

Sie hat 1660 in York, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten William Pettipoole Sr. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. April 1727 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: Ann Parham Smith(이)가 1635년 8월 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Isaac Smith(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margrett Ann Parkham(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1660년 미국 버지니아 주 요르크에서 William Pettipoole Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1727년 4월 20일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ann Parham Smith nasceu em agosto de 1635, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Isaac Smith, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margrett Ann Parkham, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com William Pettipoole Sr. em 1660, em York, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de abril de 1727, em Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ann Parham Smith родилась август 1635 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Isaac Smith было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margrett Ann Parkham, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Pettipoole Sr. в 1660 г. в местности ерк, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 20 апреля 1727 г. в местности сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 91 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ann Parham Smith nació en agosto de 1635, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Isaac Smith, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Margrett Ann Parkham, tenía 15 años. Se casó con William Pettipoole Sr. en 1660, en York, Virginia, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 20 de abril de 1727, en Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 91 años.
